channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 7.0 earthquake off Northern California prompts brief tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook a large area of Northern California on Thursday, knocking items off grocery store shelves, sending children scrambling under desks and prompting a brief tsunami warning for 5.3 million people along the U.S. West Coast.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Korea's ruling party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, boosting chances of impeachment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s ruling party chief showed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon's impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police release new photos as they search for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The masked gunman who stalked and killed the head of one of the largest U.S. health insurers had the words “deny,” “defend” and “depose” emblazoned on his ammunition, echoing a phrase used by industry critics, two law enforcement officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Yes, Chipotle is raising menu prices again: Here's why
item.description: The cost of your burrito bowl is going up. Chipotle has raised menu prices for the first time in over a year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five new details in search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's shooter
item.description: New details emerged on Thursday as the manhunt entered its second day for the suspected gunman in the fatal shooting of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: France's Macron vows to stay in office till end of term, says he'll name a new prime minister soon
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron vowed Thursday to stay in office until the end of his term, due in 2027, and announced that he will name a new prime minister within days in efforts to overcome the political deadlock following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 186 minors cited in Utah bar sting
item.description: Over 180 citations were issued to minors for being inside a Sugar House bar following an investigation into concerns of "serious violations" of Utah's alcohol laws.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police test DNA and fingerprints on discarded bottle as they hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of midtown Manhattan surveillance cameras remained at large Thursday as the nation’s largest police department hunted for him.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At a sinkhole above an abandoned Pennsylvania coal mine, hopes of a rescue dwindle
item.description: Three days after a Pennsylvania woman searching for her cat disappeared at the chasm of a three-story deep sinkhole, the digging crews were back at it Thursday, hoping for a miracle but expecting to recover her remains.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tsunami warning canceled after strong California earthquake
item.description: The National Weather Service canceled a tsunami warning Thursday for a large stretch of California coastline, saying the threat had passed following a strong earthquake near Eureka.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Teen found guilty of beating disabled DC man Reggie Brown to death sentenced to 7 years
item.description: One of the younger victims convicted of beating Washington, D.C., disabled resident Reggie Brown will be in prison until she is 21 years old following her sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students unearth mastodon fossil near St. Louis
item.description: Long before there was a Great River Road, about 27,000-and-a-half years ago, a mastodon once stood atop the bluffs overlooking the Mississippi River. Faculty and students at Principia College have discovered the specimen.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Clark, NewsNation

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: US judge rejects Boeing's plea deal in a conspiracy case stemming from fatal plane crashes
item.description: A federal judge has rejected a deal that would have let Boeing to plead guilty to a felony conspiracy charge and pay a fine for misleading U.S. regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed and 346 people died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dollar Tree could change prices, remove merchandise amid Trump's tariff plan
item.description: Dollar Tree announced they are ready to make certain changes amid President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Father of victim in NYC subway chokehold case sues defendant Daniel Penny
item.description: The father of the victim in the fatal New York City subway chokehold case has sued defendant Daniel Penny as a Manhattan jury continues deliberating.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illinois homeowner feels underground mine is splitting her house in two
item.description: About 330,000 Illinois homes sit on top of old abandoned mines. Some say their homes are sinking because of it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin left message behind to 'make a statement' or 'throw off police': Detectives
item.description: Words etched on bullet casings at the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder were left behind "to make a statement," an NYPD detective said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo government 'on alert' over mystery flu-like disease that killed dozens
item.description: “First diagnostics are leading us to think it is a respiratory disease,” an official said. “But we need to wait for the laboratory results.
item.dc:creator: Jean-Yves Kamale, KRON4

item.title: Waymo unveils plan to bring its robotaxi service to Miami
item.description: Waymo is gearing up to bring its robotaxi service to Miami which will accelerate an expansion that's been happening while its hobbled rivals remain in its rearview mirror.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Concerns were amplified last week after Musk went after retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman, who became an outspoken critic of President-elect Trump after testifying in his first impeachment trial.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ammunition from UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting had words written on it: sources
item.description: Bullets found on the ground where UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot Wednesday had writing on them, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Kala Rama, Fox 5

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 kindergarteners wounded and gunman dead after shooting at California religious school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were in “extremely critical condition” Wednesday after being shot at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and the gunman died at the scene, apparently from a self-inflicted gunshot, police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged Chinese cyberattack: FBI urges encrypted apps
item.description: Salt Typhoon, a Chinese government-linked hacking group, is one of the largest intelligence breaches in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Authorities continue investigating unusual drone activity around Trump's golf club in New Jersey: video
item.description: Officials are still investigating unusual drone activity reported in recent weeks in New Jersey. The FAA set temporary restrictions above Trump National Golf Club in Bedminster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, JONATHAN MATTISE and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: IRS warns holiday shoppers to be cautious of scams
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service and its Security Summit partners issued a warning Monday, urging taxpayers to exercise extra caution during holiday shopping to avoid falling victim to identity theft scams.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
